items.q: What is the difference between primary and secondary processing?
items.correct: Primary turns a raw food into a usable ingredient; secondary turns ingredients into food products.
items.misconception: They are the same stage with different names.
items.random_wrong: Primary processing only happens to meat.
items.why: Students fail to separate the conversion of raw materials from the making of finished products.

items.q: What does pasteurisation do to milk?
items.correct: Heats it briefly to destroy harmful bacteria, then cools it, making it safer.
items.misconception: Removes all bacteria so the milk lasts forever.
items.random_wrong: Adds extra vitamins to the milk.
items.why: Learners overestimate pasteurisation as total sterilisation rather than reducing harmful bacteria.

items.q: Why are additives used in food?
items.correct: To preserve food or improve its colour, flavour or texture.
items.misconception: Additives are always harmful chemicals with no purpose.
items.random_wrong: Additives are added only to increase the price.
items.why: Students assume all additives are dangerous, overlooking their legitimate functional roles.

items.q: Milling wheat grains into flour is an example of:
items.why: Correct answer: Primary processing. “Secondary processing” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Heating milk briefly to destroy harmful bacteria then cooling it is called:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Pasteurisation. “Fermentation” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Freezing, canning and drying are all methods of food:
items.why: Correct answer: Preservation. “Seasoning” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: An emulsifier added to mayonnaise is an example of a food:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Additive. “Nutrient” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
